scenes.voice: Hey kids, get ready to explore the world of globalisation and trade! Imagine you're shopping in a store and buying a toy made in another country. How do things get from one place to another? Let's find out!
When a country buys things from another country, it's called an import. For example, if we import apples from Australia, it means we're bringing Australian apples into our country.
On the other hand, when a country sells things to another country, it's called an export. For example, if we export toys to China, it means we're sending Chinese-made toys to China.
Some big companies, like Coca-Cola and Nike, sell their products all around the world. These are called multinational companies because they operate in many different countries.
Fair trade is when companies make sure that people who help make goods, like farmers or workers, get a fair price for their work. It's like making sure everyone gets a fair share of the treats!
What happens when a country buys things from another country? A) It imports them B) It exports them C) It produces them

Let's review what we've learned. Importing is when a country buys things from another country. Exporting is when a country sells things to another country. And multinational companies are big companies that sell their products all around the world. We also learned about fair trade, which makes sure people get a fair price for their work. Great job, kids!
